refinement_sections.content: I need to analyze how well different CPU scheduling algorithms work with a new multi-core processor. This means I have to look at how fast the CPU can process tasks, how many tasks it can handle at once, and if it treats all tasks fairly.
I will compare three scheduling algorithms. I think Round Robin, First-Come-First-Serve, and Shortest Job First are good choices because they are common.

I need to choose workloads that are realistic. This could include things like running applications that people use every day, like web browsers and word processors, and maybe some heavy tasks like video editing.

I will look at response time (how quickly tasks start), throughput (how many tasks are completed in a certain time), and fairness (if all tasks get a fair amount of CPU time). I will measure these metrics during the experiment.
I assume that the workloads will behave like they do in real life and that the system can handle the tests without crashing. I also assume that the scheduling algorithms will work as expected.

I will set up the multi-core processor and run each of the scheduling algorithms with the same workloads. Then, I will collect data on the metrics and compare the results to see which algorithm performs best.
